subject: Anthropology
title: Dendrochronology
description: A quick, practical guide to Dendrochronology.

hook.text: A tree adds one ring per year. Count the rings and you know its age. Match patterns and you can date buildings.

buildup.text: Dendrochronology dates wood by matching its ring patterns to a master sequence built from overlapping tree samples.

discovery.text: It can date wooden beams, ship timbers, and charcoal to the exact year — far more precise than radiocarbon.

twist.text: Ring width also records climate. Narrow rings mean drought. Dendrochronology is both a clock and a weather diary.

climax.text: Tree rings connect archaeology to climate history, showing how droughts and cold spells shaped human civilizations.

punchline.text: Trees recorded history before humans could write.

quiz.question: What makes dendrochronology precise?
options: It uses chemical analysis of soil
It matches tree ring patterns to date wood to exact years
